question_text: A skydiver has reached terminal velocity (constant falling speed). Which of the following is true?

options.label: A
options.text: Air resistance is much smaller than weight, so the skydiver accelerates downward

options.label: B
options.text: Weight and air resistance are balanced, so there is no resultant force

options.label: C
options.text: Weight is much larger than air resistance, causing rapid acceleration

options.label: D
options.text: There are no forces acting on the skydiver at terminal velocity

explanation: At terminal velocity, the skydiver's speed is constant, meaning acceleration is zero. This only happens when weight (downward) and air resistance (upward) are equal and balanced, giving a resultant force of zero.
